title: LATAM Patient Identity & Record Matching Crisis
problem: Patient identity management in LATAM is fragmented. No universal patient identifier. Same patient has different records at different facilities. Duplicate records common (up to 20% in some hospitals). Medical errors from mismatched records. No way to aggregate patient history across providers. This is foundational infrastructure missing across the region.
solution: Patient identity matching platform using probabilistic matching + biometrics. Creates universal patient ID within healthcare ecosystem. Links records across facilities. Patient-controlled health passport on mobile. Integration layer for EMRs to query patient history.
technology: Probabilistic record matching algorithms, biometric verification (fingerprint, facial recognition), blockchain/distributed ledger for identity, FHIR APIs, mobile app
target_customer: Ministry of Health (national identity), hospital systems, health insurers, EMR vendors
traction_signals: Every healthcare system struggles with identity
Estonia, India proved national health ID systems work
Data protection laws create compliance need
regulatory_notes: Privacy regulations (LGPD, etc.), biometric data protection, government approval for national ID systems
varden_fit: CRITICAL FIT - Patient identity is foundational infrastructure. Every other system depends on it. Government contracts possible. High technical complexity creates moat.
notes: CONTRARIAN + BOREALING: Patient identity is the most boring backend infrastructure possible. But it's critical - without it, nothing else works. Estonia proved the model. Massive government contracts possible.
